task: I need to text a coworker about covering the last hour of my shift because something came up. What’s a simple way to word it so the handoff is clear and nothing gets missed?

turns.agent_response:
Here’s a simple text you can send:

“Hey [Name], something came up and I need to step out for the last hour of my shift. Could you cover it for me? I’ll make sure everything is handed off clearly before I go.”

If you want, I can also give you:
- a more casual version
- a more professional version
- a version that includes a quick handoff checklist

user_simulator.text: Yeah, give me the handoff checklist version. Keep it short. I don’t need some polished speech — just something I can send that makes it clear what’s left, what tables to watch, and who’s already paid.

turns.agent_response:
Sure — here’s a short text you can send:

“Hey [Name], something came up and I need you to cover the last hour of my shift. I’ll leave a quick handoff: what’s left, which tables to watch, and who’s already paid. Please let me know if you need anything else before I go.”

If you want, I can make it even more casual and more like a real text message.

user_simulator.text:
Close, but make it actually say the handoff. Right now it still sounds vague.

Give me a version that names the remaining tables/checks, who’s paid, and anything to watch for — short, textable. Not a speech.

turns.agent_response:
Sure — try this:

“Hey [Name], something came up and I need you to cover the last hour. Handoff: tables [#/#] still need checks, [name(s)] have already paid, and watch for [anything to watch for]. Let me know if you need anything else before I go.”

If you want, I can turn it into a fill-in-the-blank version you can send fast.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s basically it. Make it sound like an actual table handoff, not placeholders everywhere:

“Hey [Name], something came up and I need you to cover the last hour. Handoff: tables 12 and 14 still need checks, 9 and 11 are already paid, and watch 7 in case they ask for dessert. I’ll be out in a minute.”

turns.agent_response:
That works well — it’s clear and sounds natural.

If you want, here’s a slightly smoother version:

“Hey [Name], something came up and I need you to cover the last hour. Handoff: tables 12 and 14 still need checks, 9 and 11 are already paid, and keep an eye on 7 in case they want dessert. I’ll be out in a minute.”

If you want, I can make it even more casual or more “server-text” sounding.

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that works. Clean enough, not trying too hard. I’d send it.
